name: Peanut Butter Chocolate Chip Breads
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Firmly packed light brown sugar
recipeInstructions: This recipe can be used to make mini-muffins or mini-loaves. Sift together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a bowl. In a large bowl, cream the butter and peanut butter with the brown sugar until smooth. Beat in the eggs one at a time. Add the buttermilk and beat everything until well combined. Gradually mix in the dry ingredients until just combined. Stir in the chocolate chips. Pour the batter into five buttered and floured loaf pans (5 3/4 x 3 1/4 x 2 inches). Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 40 to 45 minutes, or until a tester inserted into the center comes out clean. Remove from pans and let cool on racks. The breads can be stored wrapped tightly in plastic wrap and foil for up to one week, or frozen for up to one month. Makes five small loaves.
